mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of RSAFS monthly changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth expectations and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE falls below 5.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 5.5%) indicate an overheating economy with strong capacity utilization and pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and low UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods of rising FEDFUNDS, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200 thousand signals stronger-than-expected labor market momentum, reducing recession probability and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases by more than 200 thousand in a month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures broad economic momentum that is not fully reflected in high-frequency equity prices, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly changes for predicting 60-day S&P 500 returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low nominal federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with a rising consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) generates negative real interest rates, which incentivize risk-taking and credit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of low FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a hit rate below 55% over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1.0 percent signals a contracting manufacturing sector that drags down aggregate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines by more than 1.0 percent over three months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.
